title: Carpenter Ant Control in Edison, NJ | $350–$2,000

These wood-destroying pests can cause significant structural damage if left untreated, making professional carpenter ant control essential for protecting your property investment. Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive carpenter ant elimination services throughout Edison, with pricing ranging from $350 to $2,000 depending on infestation severity and property size.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of experience serving Edison and surrounding New Jersey communities with specialized carpenter ant expertise. Our licensed technicians use advanced detection methods and targeted treatments to eliminate colonies at the source, backed by our satisfaction guarantee and competitive pricing tailored to Edison homeowners.
localContext: Edison's diverse neighborhoods—from the historic homes near Menlo Park to the newer developments around Roosevelt Park—each present unique carpenter ant risks, especially in properties with mature trees and wooded areas. The township's humid climate and proximity to green spaces like the Edison Wetlands make it particularly susceptible to carpenter ant activity during spring and summer months.

faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Edison, NJ?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Edison typically ranges from $350 to $2,000, depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, and whether multiple colonies are present. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any treatment.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Edison home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants (up to 5/8 inch long) and are typically black or dark brown. You may notice wood shavings (frass) near baseboards, hear rustling sounds in walls, or see winged ants emerging in spring. Our Edison technicians can accurately identify the species during your inspection.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take to work in Edison homes?
faqs.answer: Most Edison homeowners see significant reduction in carpenter ant activity within 7-10 days of treatment. Complete colony elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks as the treatment reaches the queen and all satellite colonies. We schedule follow-up visits to ensure complete eradication.

faqs.question: Are carpenter ants common in Edison, NJ?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants are very common in Edison due to the area's mature tree canopy, humid climate, and mix of older wooden structures. They're particularly active from March through October and are attracted to moisture-damaged wood, making regular inspections important for Edison homeowners.
